device_flag: --device none (CPU NEON path; Adreno Vulkan dispatch is not on the shadow-cache path)
findings: Android SP hook works end-to-end on S22 Ultra — the v1.05 backend-aware gather/scatter fix lets the post-decode hook run against CPU-resident KV cleanly.
Ship 5,5,4,3 costs +0.27% PPL at essentially no throughput overhead (33.7 vs 32.4 s/pass) on the 1B model. This matches the desktop CUDA ship result (+0.45%).

sqfree+spinor 5,4,4,4,5 costs +3.5% PPL and ~1.7× per-pass time on Dolphin-1B — consistent with the scaling law floor (Dolphin 1B Q8 safe K-corr ≈ 0.988; the sqfree path pushes K-corr lower than the ship path). sqfree+spinor is a hd=128 / larger-model feature; this re-confirms the 'use ship path on smaller/more-quantised models' guidance in CLAUDE.md.

Vulkan compute path on Adreno 730 is still out of scope for the shadow cache (same shader-blob-layout limit as desktop v1.08). --device none routes everything through the CPU NEON path which is already validated at 14/14 via test_adreno.
